{{Quote|No, Niru! Those scruples are weak! Your enemies can use them against you, and so destroy all that you have worked for!|Durne misleading Niru|<ref name="Meow" />}}
'''Durne''' was a [[Cantrosian]] intelligence operative. In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], Durne was hired by the [[Nagai/Legends|Nagai]] [[Commander/Legends|commander]] [[Ozrei N'takkilomandrife|Knife]] to sow conflict between two [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]–supporting resistance groups in the [[Cantros system/Legends|Cantros system]] by convincing the members of each faction that the other was loyal to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. While this ruse resulted in open warfare and serious casualties among his [[Species/Legends|species]], Durne's plot was foiled by the [[Heroes of Yavin/Legends|Rebel heroes]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], and [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], and the two sides were reunited.

==Biography==
===Lies and sabotage===
{{Quote|We have a very brave and skillful intelligence network, General. They answer to me.|Durne to Han Solo|<ref name="Meow" />}}
[[File:Minkacampsout.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Durne (left) with Minka and Sami]]
As of [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="TNEC">"[[The Forgotten War: The Nagai and the Tofs]]" describes the events in the [[Cantros system/Legends|Cantros system]] as occurring several [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]] after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places in the first half of [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].</ref> the [[Cantrosian]]<ref name="CN2" /> Durne<ref name="Meow" /> had been hired by [[Nagai/Legends|Nagai]] [[Commander/Legends|commander]] [[Ozrei N'takkilomandrife|Knife]] to destabilize the [[Cantros system/Legends|Cantros system]]<ref name="NnT" /> in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' — Based on corresponding data for [[Cantros system/Legends|Cantros system]]</ref> and assist in paving the way for a [[Nagai expeditionary force]].<ref name="NnT" /> To this end, Durne was able to inveigle his way into two [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]–supporting resistance groups—in [[Niru's faction|one faction]], he befriended [[Niru]], while in [[Minka's faction|another]], he met [[Minka]] and [[Sami (Cantrosian)|Sami]]. After separately gaining everybody's trust, Durne became head of intelligence for both organizations. One of his ploys involved withholding the news that [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] had [[Death/Legends|died]] from Cantrosian information channels.<ref name="Meow" />

With control over this information, Durne convinced each side that the other was working for the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], and soon both factions were striving to destroy each other. Having been briefed on the situation by Minka, [[Heroes of Yavin/Legends|Rebel heroes]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], and [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] traveled to an [[Cantrosian asteroid base|asteroid base]] to investigate; while there, they met several Cantrosian fighters, including Durne. When informed by the Rebels that Imperial activity in the [[Saijo sector/Legends|sector]] was zero, Durne lied to them and said that the information gathered by his network was accurate. He was unable, however, to stop the Rebels from departing with Minka and Sami for the opposing faction's [[Niru's base|base]]. Despite claiming he would remain on the [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]], Durne traveled to the base himself.<ref name="Meow" />

===The plot unravels===
{{Quote|Kill them now before they spread dissension with their lies!|Durne to Niru|<ref name="Meow" />}}
[[File:GetTheFeathers.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Durne apprehended]]
Having located the Rebel group, Niru and her faction captured Sami, Solo, and Skywalker and brought them to the faction's base. Though Niru was against [[Torture/Legends|torturing]] or otherwise harming the alleged Imperial sympathizers, Durne attempted to persuade her to kill the [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]], but he was spotted by the Rebels. Sami and Solo appealed to Niru's followers and affirmed their allegiance to the same cause, declaring that Durne was a traitor. Durne drew a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]] and prepared to execute the group. Minka and Organa, however, arrived on the scene, having evaded capture, and Organa disarmed Durne.<ref name="Meow" />

An [[Skirmish in the Cantros system|armed exchange]] ensued, and Durne was able to escape focus. Reasoning that Niru dying would sway the group back under his influence and that the fog of battle would obscure a deliberate shot, he prepared to shoot her. Seeing this, Skywalker intercepted Durne's shot with his [[Luke Skywalker's green lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] and incapacitated the double agent. As the fighting ended and the Cantrosian factions began to reconcile, Durne attempted to flee but was stopped by Niru. With the other members of his [[Species/Legends|species]] no longer at war, Durne was bound and slated for honorable, nonviolent interrogation.<ref name="Meow" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Minka knows how glad I was to see her. I'll stay and keep an eye on things here.|Durne lying to the Rebels|<ref name="Meow" />}}
Durne showed no remorse for cultivating tensions between members of his own people or any resulting deaths, though he separately claimed loyalty to members of either faction and was persuasive enough to set the two factions against each other successfully. He was also entirely willing to kill others to fulfill his mission, first threatening to shoot Skywalker before planning to do the same to Niru. Durne had [[Color/Legends|white]] [[Hair/Legends|fur]] and hair and green [[Eye/Legends|eyes]].<ref name="Meow" />

==Equipment==
{{Quote|Fool! You've just bought yourself the first death!|Durne while brandishing his blaster at Luke Skywalker|<ref name="Meow" />}}
Durne wore a green [[Jumpsuit/Legends|jumpsuit]] and brown [[Boot/Legends|boots]], and he carried a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] pistol in a [[Holster/Legends|holster]] around his waist.<ref name="Meow" />

==Behind the scenes==
Durne appeared in the [[Marvel Comics]] issue [[Star Wars (1977) 93|''Star Wars'' (1977) 93]],<ref name="Meow" /> which was published on [[December 18]], [[1984]]<ref name="Marvel">{{BookCite|title=[[Marvel Age]]|number=Vol. 1 No. 23|publisher=[[Marvel Comics]] Group|date=[[February]] [[1985]]}}</ref> as part of the [[Star Wars (1977)|''Star Wars'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic series]] and written by [[Mary Jo Duffy]]. In the comic, Durne was drawn by [[Sal Buscema]].<ref name="Meow" />
